description:
src/lib.rs:158-175 (in get) builds a helpful 'check DOPPLER_TOKEN and project permissions' message on 401/403. src/lib.rs:236-240 (in put) does not — it falls through to map_http_status which produces a generic 'HTTP 401' or 'HTTP 403'.

Trigger: a user runs put with a token that has read but not write scope on this Doppler config. They get 'HTTP 403' with no actionable detail. Read works (they can construct the backend and call get), so debugging is misleading.

Bad behavior: user-facing error quality differs between get and put for no defensible reason.

Fix: lift the 401/403 → 'check token and permissions' branch into map_http_status itself (or a small helper auth_aware_status_to_error) and call it from both get and put. The current duplication also means future improvements to the message will drift between paths.
title: put does not produce auth-aware error on 401/403 — only get does
